deep_dive.title: Lip Forcing: Few-Step Autoregressive Diffusion for Real-time Lip Synchronization
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion-based lip synchronization models achieve strong visual quality and audio-visual alignment, but full-sequence bidirectional attention and many denoising steps make them impractical for real-time inference. We present Lip Forcing, to our knowledge the first autoregressive diffusion method for video-to-video (V2V) lip synchronization, which distills a 14B audio-conditioned bidirectional video diffusion teacher into causal students. At inference, the students generate each chunk in only two denoising steps without inference-time CFG, enabling real-time lip synchronization. A lip-sync-specific teacher-trajectory analysis reveals a CFG fidelity-sync tradeoff: no-CFG predictions favor reference fidelity, whereas CFG-guided predictions favor synchronization within a mid-trajectory band. Lip Forcing translates this finding into three analysis-derived components: Sync-Window DMD, a two-step inference schedule, and a SyncNet-based reward. We validate Lip Forcing at two student scales, both distilled from the 14B teacher. The 1.3B student crosses into real-time streaming at 31 FPS, $17.6\times$ faster than its same-scale bidirectional model. The 14B student, the largest diffusion model reported for V2V lip synchronization, runs $39.8\times$ faster than its teacher at comparable reference fidelity. Time-to-first-frame is sub-millisecond at both scales, far below every diffusion baseline.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: distills a 14B video diffusion teacher into causal students for few-step autoregressive lip-sync
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将14B视频扩散教师蒸馏为因果学生模型，实现少步自回归唇同步
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将14B视频扩散教师蒸馏为因果学生模型，实现少步自回归唇同步
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将14B视频扩散教师蒸馏为因果学生模型，实现少步自回归唇同步
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Lip Forcing distills a large bidirectional video diffusion model into causal autoregressive students that generate lip-sync in only 2 denoising steps, enabling real-time inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散唇同步模型因双向注意力和多步采样难以实时。本文提出Lip Forcing，首个将14B音频条件双向视频扩散教师蒸馏为因果学生自回归模型的方法，推理时仅用2步去噪且无需CFG，实现实时唇同步。分析发现CFG在保真度与同步性之间存在权衡，为少步蒸馏提供了新见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散唇同步模型因双向注意力和多步采样难以实时。本文提出Lip Forcing，首个将14B音频条件双向视频扩散教师蒸馏为因果学生自回归模型的方法，推理时仅用2步去噪且无需CFG，实现实时唇同步。分析发现CFG在保真度与同步性之间存在权衡，为少步蒸馏提供了新见解。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: post training distillation for video diffusion models

deep_dive.title: Mean Flow Distillation: Robust and Stable Distillation for Flow Matching Models
deep_dive.abstract: Flow Matching models have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of generative tasks. However, their reliance on ODE-based iterative sampling incurs substantial computational overhead in inference, which limits their applicability in real-time scenes. While distillation is a promising solution, existing approaches largely borrow from diffusion-based score matching, often failing to exploit the intrinsic geometric structure of flows and suffering from training instability, high variance, and degraded generation quality. In this paper, we propose Mean Flow Distillation (MFD), a novel distillation framework tailored for flow matching models. We theoretically demonstrate that MFD acts as a temporal low-pass filter, effectively suppressing the high-frequency optimization noise inherent in variational score distillation (VSD) while ensuring global trajectory consistency. We further prove the Mean Flow Matching Theorem, establishing that matching expected average velocities is sufficient for strict distribution alignment. Empirically, on challenging tasks of high-dimensional manifolds including 4D occupancy forecasting and text-to-image generation, MFD achieves state-of-the-art performance, enabling high-fidelity single-step generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: distillation for accelerating flow matching; directly applicable to video generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对流匹配模型的蒸馏方法，可迁移至视频生成加速
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对流匹配模型的蒸馏方法，可迁移至视频生成加速
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对流匹配模型的蒸馏方法，可迁移至视频生成加速
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Mean Flow Distillation (MFD), a novel distillation framework for flow matching models that acts as a temporal low-pass filter, enabling robust and stable few-step sampling while maintaining generation quality, directly benefiting video generation acceleration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有流匹配模型依赖迭代采样导致计算开销大，现有的蒸馏方法常不稳定且质量下降。本文提出均值流蒸馏（MFD），从理论上证明其作为时间低通滤波器的作用，能有效抑制蒸馏过程中的高频噪声，实现稳定且高质量的少步采样。在多种生成任务上，MFD在保持生成质量的同时大幅减少采样步数，训练过程更加鲁棒。该工作为加速扩散/流模型提供了通用蒸馏框架，可直接推广至视频生成领域。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有流匹配模型依赖迭代采样导致计算开销大，现有的蒸馏方法常不稳定且质量下降。本文提出均值流蒸馏（MFD），从理论上证明其作为时间低通滤波器的作用，能有效抑制蒸馏过程中的高频噪声，实现稳定且高质量的少步采样。在多种生成任务上，MFD在保持生成质量的同时大幅减少采样步数，训练过程更加鲁棒。该工作为加速扩散/流模型提供了通用蒸馏框架，可直接推广至视频生成领域。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: distillation methods for accelerating video generation while maintaining quality

quick_skim.title: Parallel Jacobi Decoding for Fast Autoregressive Image Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) models have demonstrated remarkable performance in generating high-fidelity images. However, their inherently sequential next-token prediction leads to significantly slower inference. Recent studies have introduced Jacobi-style decoding to accelerate autoregressive image generation. Extending the draft sequence initially improves efficiency, yet the acceleration quickly saturates as error propagation in the one-dimensional sequence hinders convergence. Observing that images exhibit strong local spatial correlations, we propose Parallel Jacobi Decoding (PJD), a training-free decoding approach that expands draft tokens in the two-dimensional spatial domain to enable efficient spatially parallel refinement. PJD adjusts the attention mask to mitigate error accumulation and improve convergence stability. Extensive experiments on diverse datasets show that PJD achieves 4.8x-6.4x acceleration across multiple autoregressive image generation models while maintaining competitive generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parallel Jacobi Decoding accelerates autoregressive image generation via spatially parallel refinement, reducing sequential steps
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 并行雅可比解码通过空间并行细化加速自回归图像生成，减少顺序步骤
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 并行雅可比解码通过空间并行细化加速自回归图像生成，减少顺序步骤
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 并行雅可比解码通过空间并行细化加速自回归图像生成，减少顺序步骤
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PJD expands draft tokens in 2D spatial domain for efficient parallel refinement, significantly reducing inference steps in autoregressive image generation while maintaining quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归图像生成速度受限于顺序预测。本文提出并行雅可比解码，利用图像局部空间相关性，在二维空间域扩展草稿令牌，实现空间并行细化，克服一维序列的误差传播，大幅减少解码步数。在ImageNet上取得加速。方法仅需调整注意力掩码，无需训练。该方案可迁移到视频自回归生成，实现少步采样。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 自回归图像生成速度受限于顺序预测。本文提出并行雅可比解码，利用图像局部空间相关性，在二维空间域扩展草稿令牌，实现空间并行细化，克服一维序列的误差传播，大幅减少解码步数。在ImageNet上取得加速。方法仅需调整注意力掩码，无需训练。该方案可迁移到视频自回归生成，实现少步采样。
